items.title: Anthropic suspended Fable and Mythos access
items.oneliner: The US forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals, making model access a geopolitical dependency.

items.title: OpenAI bought Ona for Codex workspaces
items.oneliner: OpenAI bought Ona to expand Codex workspaces, signaling that agent tooling is becoming a product surface, not a demo.

items.title: Anthropic reversed hidden safeguard behavior
items.oneliner: Anthropic walked back Claude visibility safeguards after researcher backlash, showing stealthy safety interventions now carry real product risk.

items.title: FFmpeg exposed twenty one zero days
items.oneliner: Researchers found 21 FFmpeg zero days, a reminder that media pipelines still hide remote code execution landmines.

items.title: Malware abused safety refusals to hide
items.oneliner: Spyware authors stuffed nuclear and bioweapons text into samples to trigger AI refusals and blind security scanners.

items.title: Arch AUR malware spread across packages
items.oneliner: Arch Linux is cleaning up a mass AUR compromise that hit hundreds, then 1,500-plus packages, exposing open source supply chain fragility.

items.title: BlackCore faces election meddling scrutiny
items.oneliner: Reuters reports BlackCore is suspected in New York and Scotland vote meddling, pushing AI influence operations back into focus.

items.title: Apple moved font parsing to Swift
items.oneliner: Apple rewrote its TrueType hinting interpreter in Swift and got a 13 percent speedup with better memory safety.

items.title: WASI 0.3 shipped native async components
items.oneliner: WASI 0.3 bakes async into the component model, removing per component event loop hacks and making WebAssembly services saner.

items.title: Postgres 19 is adding temporal tables
items.oneliner: Postgres 19 is finally getting native temporal tables, eliminating years of trigger heavy workarounds for historical queries.
